metadata.title: Extending Kernel PCA through Dualization: Sparsity, Robustness and Fast Algorithms

sections.title: Kernel PCA Problem
content: Given:n datapoints (xi) in X, feature map φ: X → H to a Hilbert space H.
Goal:find s directions in H that maximize the variance under orthonormal conditions. The KPCA optimization problem is
We use the following definitions:
The Stiefel manifold of orthonormal s-frames in H.
G(W) is the matrix such that G(W)ij = (wi, wj).
Γ: H^s → R^{n×s} is the linear operator s.t. for all (i, j).
G is the Gram matrix G = [k(xi, xj)], where k is the positive definite kernel function.
The usual way to solve (1) is through SVD of G, which is slow with larger n.

sections.title: Difference of convex functions
content: Key idea:Rewrite (1) as a difference of convex functions
Two key advantages:
Allows new gradient-based algorithm to solve KPCA efficiently without the SVD of G.
It becomes possible to slightly modify the loss function f to enforce specific properties such as robustness or sparsity.
Proposition 0.1(Dual of difference of convex functions). Let U, K be two Hilbert spaces, g: U → R and f: K → R be two convex lower semi-continuous functions and Γ ∈ L(U, K).

sections.title: Faster KPCA with Gradient Descent
content: Motivation for going from primal to dual:we show that g*(Γ^T H) is related to the nuclear norm of some low dimensional matrix.
Proposition 0.2.Let g be the indicator function of the Stiefel manifold and Γ as in Problem 1. Then for all H ∈ R^{n×s}:
The computational complexity of computing the gradient of π:
Computation of H^T G H in O(sn²).
SVD of H^T G H in O(s³).
KPCA Training Timefor multiple KPCA problems with fixed δ = 10⁻² accuracy. Speedup factor w.r.t. RSVD.
•Influence of the number of components son training time: higher s leads to longer training times.

tables.description: Table comparing KPCA training time across SVD, Lanczos, RSVD, and our proposed method for different datasets.

figures.description: Line chart showing training time vs number of components s for LBFGS and RSVD methods.

sections.title: Beyond variance maximization
content: Typical loss function: square loss f = 1/2 ||·||²_F.
Problems:sensible to outliers, no sparsity.
Key idea:use a loss obtained with infimal convolution
where Ψ is a well-chosen function that enforces robustness or sparsity.

sections.title: DC Optimization
content: As f is a Moreau envelope, its gradient is always defined for all Y ∈ R^{n×s}.

sections.title: Robustness and sparsity
content: Denoting ||·||_* as the dual norm of ||·|| and the balls of radius t for these norms as B*_t and B_t, we extend KPCA with Huber and ε-insensitive objectives.
Extended Huber loss H_κ:
• Effect of κ for the losses on contaminated Iris dataset.
Extended ε-insensitive loss l_ε:
• Reconstruction error for the l_∞ loss for multiple ε and s.

figures.description: Plot showing the effect of kappa on Mean Squared Error (MSE) for different Huber losses.
Heatmaps and line plots showing reconstruction error ratio and sparsity percentage for various epsilon and s values.
